hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_title: Asking and Answering Like a Professional -- Question Types and Active Listening
lesson.menu_desc: Ask polite, indirect questions and answer with active listening at an international meeting or panel, using openers like 'Could you tell me...' and 'I wonder if...' plus confirming and follow-up moves -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 19 -- Asking Like a Professional
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 19 -- Asking Like a Professional
lesson.grammar_point: professional question types and active listening (indirect questions)

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. I am the moderator who facilitates this panel. Here is one tip for our guests. When you want more information, ask a polite question. Do not say 'Where is the room?'. Say 'Could you tell me where the room is?'. This is an indirect question, and it sounds professional. When you want to check a yes or no answer, say 'I wonder if you have the report.'. Listen with attention, confirm what you hear, and then ask a follow-up. A good inquiry and a good response make a strong panel. Thank you.

Hello. Here is a simple tip about active listening. Listening is not only about hearing words. It is about showing the other person that you understand. First, give a short response, like 'I see' or 'That makes sense.'. Then, confirm the idea in your own words: 'So, if I understand correctly, the launch is in June.'. If you are not sure, ask a polite follow-up. This is how you show respect and get better feedback. When you listen well, people trust you. Good luck!

extra_audio.text: First, I wait for the moderator to give me the turn. Then, I ask my inquiry with a polite, indirect question. Next, I listen with attention to the whole response. After that, I confirm the main idea in my own words. Finally, I ask a follow-up and thank the panel for their feedback.
